description:
Bring name->Ty resolution in-house as a PURE READ over the in-house type-expr (.11) against the captured namespace. Structure (list, union, tuple, arrow) maps via compiler2 own Types; a name classifies against the namespace captured at the declaration point -- a Type binding -> TypeName -> read the already-present TypeDefined; a builtin -> scalar minted directly with inner; an unbound lowercase name -> a free type var. No env, no ModuleTypeEnv -- the captured namespace IS the resolution context, prelude types visible by structural sharing.

It never blocks, never demands, never mints a reference -- the index-time reference walk (.1) already minted the names and the consumer (.4) already waited on their TypeDefined before calling in. A name not present here is a consumer-wiring bug, not a resolver retry. Output is crate::specs::ResolvedSpec (params: Ty + param_shapes: ResolvedTypeShape) so the shared specs scheme engine is fed unchanged.

Verify: resolver unit tests over the worked examples (foo(SomeModule.t(float),integer); a plain alias; a refines brand; (a,b)->{a,b} free vars), each handed a prebuilt namespace + TypeDefined set -- proving resolution is a pure function of captured namespace + present facts.

comments.text: Absorbed into fz-rh2.12.2: the core TypeExpr->Ty resolver and DeriveTypeDef are one unit (the TypeDefined fact closes the loop), so they land together. resolve_spec->ResolvedSpec rides the same core. Closed as merged, not done.

title: In-house type-expr resolver: name -> hard Ty via namespace + TypeDefined
